//ApiBaseUrl: Intentionally absent. Hosted scenarios (incl. Azure App Service) fall back to HostEnvironment.BaseAddress so the WASM client calls the API on the same origin. Standalone dev (Blazor DevServer at :5008) gets its value from appsettings.Development.json.
//AzureAd: MSAL config for Entra External ID. Authority/ClientId aren't secrets — they ship in the publicly downloadable WASM bundle anyway — but they identify the SPA registration. DefaultScope is the API scope we expose via 'Expose an API' on the same registration (api://<client-id>/access_as_user); the bare ID-token-only state can't call our API. ValidateAuthority=true keeps MSAL from trusting an attacker-supplied Authority.

AzureAd.//Authority: MSAL.js auto-appends /v2.0/.well-known/openid-configuration for discovery — do NOT include /v2.0 in this URL or the discovery request becomes /v2.0/v2.0/... and 404s. The API's Auth__Authority (App Service env var) is different: JwtBearer reads it as-is and appends only /.well-known/openid-configuration, so that one keeps /v2.0.
